title: Ancient History Signals
summary: Ancient history signals track new finds, new methods, and debates about how the deep past should be interpreted.

items.name: Ancient DNA keeps reshaping migration stories
items.summary: Genetic evidence is revising assumptions about population movement.
items.whyItMatters: The ancient record is becoming more scientific.

items.name: New site discovery methods are accelerating
items.summary: Remote sensing and satellite work are finding hidden settlements.
items.whyItMatters: The map of the ancient world keeps expanding.

items.name: Museum digitisation vendors
items.summary: Collections are becoming more searchable and visible online.
items.whyItMatters: Digital access changes who can study the ancient world.

items.name: Educational media platforms
items.summary: Ancient history now lives across video and web platforms.

items.name: Museums
items.summary: Artefacts and exhibitions shape public memory of antiquity.
items.whyItMatters: The institution decides what the public sees and values.

items.name: Universities
items.summary: Ancient history is still mostly anchored in academic study.

items.name: Mary Beard
items.whyItMatters: She keeps the ancient world legible to broad audiences.

items.name: David Wengrow
items.summary: Important voice on early civilisation and social complexity.

items.name: How global should ancient history be?
items.summary: The field is shifting beyond Greek and Roman centrality.
items.whyItMatters: A broader map changes what counts as normal civilisation.

items.name: How much can we infer from fragments?
items.whyItMatters: Ancient history is always balancing evidence and hypothesis.
